summary: Retested the four (effectively five) regression bullets after fixes to seedTransactions createdAt, effectiveIncome formula, AddTransactionDialog scroll container, and removal of native required. All five bullets PASS on https://budget-dash-3d.preview.emergentagent.com after localStorage.clear() + reload. (1) Seed loads with Total Spent ₹54,040 / 31 transactions, Available Balance ₹20,960 (27.9% of income remaining), Savings Rate 27.9% Healthy, MTD '100.1% used'. (2) Adding expense ₹250 Food note 'Zomato dinner TEST' via FAB places the new row as [data-testid='recent-list'] li:first-child (text starts with 'Z\nZomato dinner TEST'); Available drops to ₹20,710 (27.6%). (3) Adding Income ₹5,000 (Freelance) increases Available from ₹20,710 → ₹25,710 and Savings Rate from 27.6% → 32.1% — both cards re-render correctly. (4) Submitting with an empty amount triggers the sonner toast 'Enter a valid amount' and the dialog stays open (native required was removed so the JS toast handler fires). (5) At a 380x600 narrow viewport the submit button is visible and clickable inside the scrollable outer container — clicking it with a valid amount closes the dialog and persists the transaction. Note: CountUp animation takes ~2s to settle after mount, so initial reads can show transient sub-final values; tests polled until stabilized.
critical_code_review_comments: store.js: monotonic seedSeq makes seed createdAt strictly older than any later addTransaction by setting createdAt = midnight + sequence-microseconds; works correctly today but is technically not microsecond-accurate because ISO string truncates to ms (seedSeq is added as ms). With 31+27 = 58 seed items, createdAt spans midnight..midnight+57ms which is still strictly < any new Date() call after page load, so the ordering invariant holds. If seedSeq ever exceeded 999, two seed items could share the same second/ms boundary — fine for current dataset.
store.js: 'available = (monthlyIncome + mIncome) - mExpense' and 'effectiveIncome = monthlyIncome + mIncome' correctly fold income transactions into both Available and Savings Rate cards.

AddTransactionDialog.jsx: outer fixed-inset overflow-y-auto wrapper with the form using my-10 + mx-auto is correct — the form is now in the document flow inside a scrollable container, so on short viewports the user can scroll to reach the submit button. Verified at 380x600.
AddTransactionDialog.jsx: native 'required' removed from amount and date inputs; JS toast branch is now reachable for the empty-amount case. Verified.

Minor: outer scroll container has an onClick={onClose} that could close the dialog when the user clicks inside the padded margin around the form. Today this is prevented by the form's onClick stopPropagation, so it's fine — but worth noting that scrolling/touch-drag near the edges could feel fragile.
seed_data_creation: Used page.evaluate("localStorage.clear()") + reload to get fresh seed for the run. Added one TEST expense (Zomato dinner TEST ₹250 Food) and one TEST income (Freelance TEST ₹5,000) and one stray ₹100 from the narrow-viewport submit test. No cleanup performed in-app; will reset on next localStorage.clear().
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 19 originally-spec'd behaviors plus the 5 retest bullets are now green. App is frontend-only at https://budget-dash-3d.preview.emergentagent.com. To re-run, always: page.evaluate('localStorage.clear()') then reload, wait ~2.5s for CountUp animations to settle before reading stat-card text. data-testids in use: total-spent-card, available-balance-card, savings-rate-card, savings-rate-value, fab-add-transaction, add-transaction-dialog, amount-input, note-input, date-input, type-expense-button, type-income-button, category-{id}, submit-transaction-button, recent-list, add-transaction-scroll-container.
rca of the issue: No new RCA needed — previously identified bugs were both fixed: (a) Recent ordering: seed createdAt is now midnight + monotonic seedSeq offset (ms), which is strictly less than any wall-clock createdAt produced by addTransaction at runtime, so new tx always sorts to the top of the desc-by-createdAt list. (b) Income vs Available/SavingsRate: store.js now computes effectiveIncome = monthlyIncome + mIncome and uses it in both available and savingsRate, so income transactions update both cards. (c) Empty-amount toast: native required removed from <input type='number'>, so JS submit handler reaches the toast.error('Enter a valid amount') branch and returns without closing the dialog. (d) Narrow viewport: AddTransactionDialog now wraps the form in a fixed-inset overflow-y-auto container with the form positioned via my-10 mx-auto, so on short viewports the submit button is reachable via scroll.
